channel.title: ainews — Karl's daily AI news
channel.description: Daily AI news, deduped + tagged for hotloop's workflows

item.title: How to Ground a Korean AI Agent in Real Demographics with Synthetic Personas
item.description: Guide on building Korean AI agents using NVIDIA Nemotron with synthetic personas to ensure demographic accuracy and cultural relevance in AI systems.

item.title: Moonshot Kimi K2.6: world's leading Open Model refreshes to catch up to Opus 4.6
item.description: Moonshot released Kimi K2.6, a 1T-parameter MoE open model with 32B active parameters, achieving strong performance on coding and agent benchmarks. Capabilities in long-horizon execution and multi-agent coordination.

item.title: Maximizing Memory Efficiency to Run Bigger Models on NVIDIA Jetson
item.description: Technical guide covering quantization, flash attention, and KV cache management techniques to deploy multi-billion-parameter generative AI models on memory-constrained NVIDIA Jetson edge devices.

item.title: Run High-Throughput Reinforcement Learning Training with End-to-End FP8 Precision
item.description: NVIDIA details end-to-end FP8 precision training for LLM reinforcement learning using GRPO, addressing GPU utilization bottlenecks during high-intensity RL phases with significant throughput improvements.

item.title: Leaked deck reveals OpenAI ad partner selling ChatGPT placements by prompt relevance
item.description: StackAdapt, an OpenAI advertising partner, is selling ChatGPT ad placements targeted by user query relevance according to a leaked internal deck. This marks a new monetization phase for LLM chat interfaces through contextual advertising.

item.title: OpenAI rolls out Chronicle for Codex on macOS
item.description: OpenAI introduced Chronicle, a feature that builds memories from screen captures to enhance Codex's contextual awareness. Currently a research preview for Pro subscribers on macOS.

item.title: Amazon invests $25B more in Anthropic, total now $33B
item.description: Amazon committed an additional $25 billion investment in Anthropic, bringing total investment to $33 billion. Anthropic pledged to spend over $100 billion on AWS infrastructure over the next decade.

item.title: Microsoft adjusts GitHub Copilot plans and pricing — Opus 4.7 restricted to Pro+
item.description: Microsoft paused new signups for GitHub Copilot Pro, Pro+, and Student tiers while tightening usage limits. Opus models removed from Pro and Opus 4.7 restricted to Pro+ tier only.

item.title: AI Resistance is growing: anti-AI pushback worth tracking
item.description: Roundup of organized resistance to AI deployments across creative industries, academic institutions, and labor movements, with 150+ HN points. Covers key campaigns, boycotts, and legislation attempts targeting AI systems.

item.title: Microsoft plans token-based billing for GitHub Copilot
item.description: Internal documents reveal Microsoft intends to shift GitHub Copilot from request-based to token-based billing. The week-over-week cost to run it has nearly doubled since January.

item.title: Reading today's open-closed performance gap
item.description: Examines how the performance gap between open and closed AI models is misunderstood when reduced to a single benchmark score, exploring how benchmark evolution, model capabilities, and training regimes interact over time.

item.title: Training Transformers to solve 95% failure rate of Cancer Trials — Noetik
item.description: Noetik uses transformers trained on spatial transcriptomics to predict patient responses to cancer treatments. Their TARIO-2 model addresses a core bottleneck where 95% of cancer drugs fail clinical trials despite being effective for specific patient populations.

item.title: Deezer says 44% of songs uploaded to its platform daily are AI-generated
item.description: The music streaming service reports that nearly half of daily song uploads are machine-generated, reflecting widespread adoption in music production.

item.title: Adobe Agents Unlock Breakthrough Creative Intelligence With NVIDIA and WPP
item.description: NVIDIA, Adobe, and WPP collaborate on agentic AI for enterprise marketing. Adobe's creative platforms + WPP's expertise + NVIDIA's Nemotron models and OpenShell runtime enable intelligent systems that plan, create, produce and activate content continuously while maintaining brand safety.

item.title: Import AI 454: Automating alignment research; Huawei HiFloat4; Kimi K2.5 safety study
item.description: Anthropic demonstrates autonomous AI agents conducting alignment research that outperform human researchers (97% recovery vs 23%). Also covers Huawei's HiFloat4 training format outperforming Western MXFP4, and safety evaluation of Chinese model Kimi K2.5 showing fewer CBRN refusals than Western counterparts.

item.title: Atlassian enables default data collection to train AI
item.description: Atlassian activates automatic data gathering from users to support artificial intelligence model development.

item.title: NVIDIA and Partners Showcase the Future of AI-Driven Manufacturing at Hannover Messe
item.description: NVIDIA demonstrates AI-powered manufacturing innovations including factory digital twins, vision AI agents, and humanoid robots. Partners show how accelerated computing, AI physics, agents and robotics are powering industrial innovation.

item.title: Claude Token Counter, now with model comparisons
item.description: Tool upgraded to compare token counts across Claude models. Opus 4.7 uses roughly 1.46x the number of tokens as Opus 4.6 on system prompts.

item.title: OpenAI helps Hyatt advance AI among colleagues
item.description: Hyatt deploys ChatGPT Enterprise across its global workforce, utilizing GPT-5.4 and Codex to enhance productivity, operations, and guest experiences.

item.title: Headless everything for personal AI
item.description: Analysis of emerging trend toward API-first services designed for AI agents rather than GUI-based user interfaces, reshaping SaaS pricing models.

item.title: Changes in the system prompt between Claude Opus 4.6 and 4.7
item.description: Detailed analysis of system prompt modifications including expanded child safety instructions, improved tool search capabilities, and reduced verbosity guidance.

item.title: Claude system prompts as a git timeline
item.description: Research project converting Anthropic's published system prompts into a git repository with fake commit dates to track changes across Claude model versions.

item.title: The Two Sides of OpenClaw
item.description: Analysis contrasting OpenClaw's public narrative of breakthrough AI agents with engineering realities of unprecedented security incidents and scaling challenges as the fastest-growing open source project in history.

item.title: Anthropic Claude Opus 4.7 — literally one step better than 4.6 in every dimension
item.description: Claude Opus 4.7 launched with improvements across coding, vision, and reasoning tasks. Supports images up to 3.75 megapixels (3x larger than prior versions), introduces a new xhigh reasoning effort level, and demonstrates approximately 11-point gains on SWE-Bench Pro.

item.title: Ecom-RLVE: Adaptive Verifiable Environments for E-Commerce Conversational Agents
item.description: Introduces Ecom-RLVE, an adaptive verifiable environment framework designed for training and evaluating e-commerce conversational agents with real-world scenarios.

item.title: Transformers to MLX: efficient inference on Apple Silicon
item.description: Discusses converting Transformers models to MLX format, enabling efficient inference on Apple Silicon devices.

item.title: Training and Finetuning Multimodal Embedding & Reranker Models with Sentence Transformers
item.description: Comprehensive guide on training multimodal embedding and reranker models using Sentence Transformers library for improved semantic search and ranking tasks.

item.title: My bets on open models, mid-2026
item.description: Details key beliefs about open model development including technical strength of open labs, focus on benchmark scores versus robustness, dominance in repetitive automation tasks, and funding challenges for Chinese open-weight companies.

item.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS: the next generation of expressive AI speech
item.description: Google DeepMind introduced a new audio model with granular audio tags that give precise control for directing AI-generated speech with enhanced expressiveness.

item.title: Rethinking AI TCO: Why Cost per Token Is the Only Metric That Matters
item.description: NVIDIA argues enterprises should evaluate AI infrastructure on cost per token rather than compute cost or FLOPS per dollar. Blackwell delivers 35x lower cost per million tokens compared to Hopper.
